name: Risotto with Prociutto and Lemon
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Paper-Thin Slices Of Prosciutto -- chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch lemon peel in a small saucepan of boiling water for 15 seconds. Drain. Repeat blanching process twice using fresh water each time. Alternatively, place the peel in a heat-proof bowl, pour boiling water over and let stand 15-20 seconds. Repeat.
2. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large heavy saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and half the prosciutto; sauté 2 minutes. Remove garlic and discard. Add rice and stir 1 minute. Add 1/2 cup wine and cook until evaporated, stirring constantly, about 5 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, bring chicken stock to a simmer in a saucepan. Reduce heat to low. Cover and keep warm. Add 1/2 cup stock to rice; reduce heat and simmer until liquid is absorbed, stirring frequently. Continue adding remaining stock a half cup at a time in the same manner, allowing each addition to be completely absorbed before adding the next half cup. This should take about 25 minutes.
4. Stop adding stock if the rice begins looking really mushy. It should be tender but still slightly firm to the bite. Remove from heat. Mix in remaining 2 tablespoons butter, then the Parmesan, lemon peel and remaining prosciutto. Garnish with paper-thin slices of lemon and/or a few julienne slices of prosciutto, if desired.
